claim: ADR-0001 declares `superseded-by: 0099`, but no ADR numbered 0099 exists in the log.
consequence: A supersession pointer to nothing leaves the reader unable to find the decision that replaced this one — the status graph is broken at this node.

claim: Supersession cycle detected: ADR-0002 -> ADR-0003 -> ADR-0002.
consequence: A cycle in the status graph means no ADR in the loop is the final, currently-governing decision.

claim: ADR-0004 and ADR-0005 are both Accepted and each claims to supersede the other.
consequence: Mutual supersession between two live decisions leaves no single decision in force.

claim: ADR-0006 (Accepted) claims to supersede the later ADR-0007, which is still Accepted.
consequence: An earlier decision cannot supersede a later one that was never updated — one status is stale.
